ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of the sensor (e.g., force, torque) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the sensor data (e.g., N, Nm) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model number of the sensor */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "systems" (
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system */,
  "system_name" TEXT /* Name of the system */,
  "system_type" TEXT /* Type of the system (e.g., robotic arm, mechanical gripper) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the system */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the system was installed */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_points" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that collected the data */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system being monitored */,
  "elapsed_time" REAL /* Elapsed time (in seconds) when the data was collected */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the data point */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data point (e.g., good, bad, uncertain) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "force_data" (
  "force_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each force data point */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that collected the data */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system being monitored */,
  "elapsed_time" REAL /* Elapsed time (in seconds) when the data was collected */,
  "force_x" REAL /* X-component of the force data */,
  "force_y" REAL /* Y-component of the force data */,
  "force_z" REAL /* Z-component of the force data */,
  "force_magnitude" REAL /* Magnitude of the force data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("force_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_force_data_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_force_data_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "torque_data" (
  "torque_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each torque data point */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that collected the data */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system being monitored */,
  "elapsed_time" REAL /* Elapsed time (in seconds) when the data was collected */,
  "torque_x" REAL /* X-component of the torque data */,
  "torque_y" REAL /* Y-component of the torque data */,
  "torque_z" REAL /* Z-component of the torque data */,
  "torque_magnitude" REAL /* Magnitude of the torque data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("torque_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_torque_data_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_torque_data_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis result */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system being analyzed */,
  "analysis_type" TEXT /* Type of analysis performed (e.g., trend analysis, anomaly detection) */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the analysis */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the result (e.g., 0-1) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the analysis was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_results_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, administrator) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_system_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access control entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  "last_access" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_system_access_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_system_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_config" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration entry */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration parameter */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration parameter */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the configuration parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the configuration parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_config_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_history" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance activity */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system being maintained */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date the maintenance was performed */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the maintenance activity */,
  "performed_by" TEXT /* Name of the person who performed the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_history_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_calibration" (
  "calibration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each calibration activity */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor being calibrated */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the calibration was performed */,
  "calibration_result" TEXT /* Result of the calibration (e.g., passed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("calibration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_calibration_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)
